revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Furumiya Kofun]][[ja:古宮古墳 (大分市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Furumiya Kofun | native_name =和泉黄金塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Furumiya Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Furumiya Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Furumiya Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.227|N|131.587667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oita|qid=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs ={{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|13|37.2|N|131|35|15.6|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Furumiya Kofun'''|古宮古墳|}} is an {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Matsubara Dentarukuri Nikku neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita| Ōita]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192172|title=古宮古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Furumiya Kofun is located in the southwest part of Ōita city, on the southern slope of a low hill overlooking a small valley created by the Bishamon River. It is a {{nihongo|hofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}}-style square tumulus with a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] as its main interior, measuring 12.45 meters from north-to-south, 12.15 meters from east-to-west, and approximately 4.9 meters in height when viewed from the south side of the front, but since it was built on a slope, the shape seen from the front and side of the tomb is different. It faces the river to the south and the mountains behind it, so the influence of [[:en:Feng Shui|Feng Shui]] can be seen.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber was made by hollowing out a huge block of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} with a total length of about 2.5 meters, width of 1.65 meters, and height of 1.77 meters, and has a corridor made of cut tuff stones. This style is often seen in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], but there is no other example in Kyushu. The stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} is also carved out of tuff and measures 2.02 x 0.79 meters, and 0.85 meters high. The side walls are made up of two thick tuff slabs, with a monolithic rock ceiling stone. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as the lid of a {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar and the legs of a small Sue pottery ceremonial stand were unearthed. All of the pieces are small fragments, and it is unclear whether they actually belong to this tumulus, but date from the mid to late 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Judging from the date of construction and the style of the burial chamber similar to that of nobility in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka, Nara}}, the person buried was a member of the Oita-no-Kimi clan, who ruled Oita County at the time. It is presumed that he was Ōita no Kimiesaka, who is recorded in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as being active on the side of Prince Hito ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)}} *[https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o205/documents/hurumiyakohunn.pdf Oita city home page]{{in lang|ja}} Furumiya Kofun<
{{Kofun navbar}}
